rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical cascade sulfonylation/cyclization of N-aryl 2-vinylbenzamides with sodium sulfinates to access 3-sulfonylisoindolin-1-ones
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of sulfinate anion (ArSO2-) to sulfonyl radical (ArSO2•); cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: Benzylic C-radical D from sulfonyl addition to styrenyl amide
Cyclized cationic iminol intermediate F (after anodic SET)

electron_flow: At the anode, sulfinate (2b) loses one electron to give sulfonyl radical C (via resonance forms A/B); C adds across the alkene of 1a to form benzylic radical D, whose amide resonance form E undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto nitrogen. A second anodic SET gives cation F, which loses H+ to deliver the isoindolin-1-one product 3ab; the cathode produces H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 3 equiv TEMPO under standard conditions suppressed product formation (only trace 3aa); supports radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Sulfonyl-DPE adduct 4 detected by NMR in 68%; product 3aa only trace — confirms free sulfonyl radical

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of substrates
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation potentials measured (ESI) consistent with anodic oxidation of sulfinate

notes: Gram-scale (3.5 mmol, 1.05 g of 1a) reaction delivered 3aa in 70% yield, supporting scalability. No DFT or KIE performed; mechanism is proposed by analogy to prior radical sulfonylation literature.
